title: Why your tickets sit unanswered (and what fixes it)
excerpt: It usually isn't laziness or understaffing — it's a routing problem nobody is measuring. Here's the single metric we audit on every new client takeover and how to fix it in a week.

children.text: When a prospect tells us their last MSP kept losing tickets, nine times out of ten it isn't laziness or understaffing. It's a routing problem nobody on the floor is measuring. The ticket came in, nobody owned the category, and it sat in a shared queue until someone noticed the client was angry.
The metric that exposes the problem

The number we audit first is time-to-assignment: how long from ticket creation until a human owner is named on the ticket. Not first reply. Not resolution. Assignment. Once you have a clean median and p95 on that number, broken out by category — security, M365, hardware, network — the routing failures fall out of the data on their own. Almost every failure is a category with no clear default owner.
How to fix routing in a week

Pull the last 90 days of tickets and tag each one with a category. For each category, identify a primary owner and a backup — write it down somewhere everyone reads. Stand up an unowned queue that someone is responsible for. Re-measure time-to-assignment after two weeks. For the clients we run today, median time-to-assignment sits around 6 minutes during business hours. None of that requires a 24/7 SOC. It requires a category owner and a queue nobody is allowed to ignore.

seoTitle: Why your tickets sit unanswered — and what fixes it
seoDescription: A routing problem, not a staffing problem. Learn the single metric MSPs should measure and how to halve your time-to-assignment in a week without hiring.

title: Field notes: the VLAN clean-up at a 42-seat law firm
excerpt: A flat network with 42 seats, two servers, and guest WiFi on the same broadcast domain. Here's what we found, what we changed, and why the project took three evenings instead of three weeks.

children.text: The firm had been with their previous MSP for six years. The network had never been segmented. Guest WiFi, staff laptops, the file server, and the practice-management database were all on the same flat broadcast domain — one misconfigured device away from a lateral movement event that would have been very expensive to explain to the state bar.

The existing firewall was capable of VLAN-aware routing but had never been configured for it. The switches were managed hardware — also unconfigured. Guest SSID was bridged directly to the LAN. We scoped a four-VLAN design: staff, servers, guest, and a management VLAN for the network gear itself. The bill of changes was 14 lines. Implementation was three evenings to avoid disrupting billable hours.
What changed — and what the firm noticed

The guest network is now isolated. Staff traffic never touches the server VLAN directly — only via the firewall policy, which is logged. The practice-management vendor's own network requirements are now met. The firm's cyber-insurance application went from a question mark on network segmentation to a checked box. Total cost was less than two months of the previous MSP's managed fee.

seoTitle: Field notes: VLAN clean-up at a 42-seat law firm
seoDescription: How we segmented a flat law-firm network in three evenings — what we found, how we scoped it, and what the firm's cyber-insurance carrier said afterward.

title: A 60-day path to SOC 2 readiness for service firms
excerpt: Most service firms are further along than they think. Here's the 60-day sequence we run with clients to get from scattered controls to an auditor-ready evidence pack.

children.text: SOC 2 readiness sounds like an enterprise project. For a 30-50 seat professional services firm, it doesn't have to be. The controls that satisfy most Type I auditors — access management, encryption at rest, incident response, change management, monitoring — are controls a well-run MSP is already enforcing. The gap is usually documentation, not implementation.

Days 1-14: gap assessment. We map your current controls against the SOC 2 Trust Services Criteria, score each control as implemented, partial, or missing, and prioritize the gaps by audit risk. Days 15-45: remediation. We close the high-risk gaps — usually access reviews, log retention, and a documented incident-response plan. Days 46-60: evidence collection. We package the artifacts the auditor needs: access logs, policy documents, configuration screenshots, and a controls matrix. At day 60 you have a defensible posture and a binder the auditor can open.

Most firms are already 60-70% of the way there. The biggest surprises are almost always the same: access reviews haven't been done in over a year, offboarding checklists exist on paper but weren't run for the last three departures, and log retention is set to 30 days when the auditor wants 90. None of these are hard to fix. They just need to be fixed before the auditor shows up, not during.

seoTitle: A 60-day path to SOC 2 readiness for service firms
seoDescription: The 60-day sequence for getting a professional services firm from scattered controls to an auditor-ready SOC 2 evidence pack — without enterprise overhead.

title: Terms of service

children.text: We provide IT services per your signed MSA / SOW. You give us access and pay invoices net-15. We protect your data, document what we do, and you can leave with 30 days' notice. Disputes are settled in Utah.
The detail below controls. The summary is a courtesy.

By accessing the Prime Hour Tech website ("Site") or engaging Prime Hour Tech LLC ("PHT," "we," "us") for any services, you agree to be bound by these Terms & Conditions ("Terms") and our Privacy Policy. If you do not agree, do not use the Site or our services.

These Terms supplement — and do not replace — any signed Master Service Agreement (MSA), Statement of Work (SOW), or Client SLA in effect between you and PHT. In the event of a conflict, the signed agreement controls.

Prime Hour Tech offers managed IT services, cybersecurity, cloud and Microsoft 365 administration, IT projects, vCIO advisory, and web services to small and mid-size businesses, primarily in Utah and remotely across the United States.
The specific scope, deliverables, response targets, and pricing for any engagement are defined in a separately executed SOW or MSA. Nothing on this Site constitutes a binding offer of service.

You must be at least 18 years old and authorized to enter into contracts on behalf of your organization to engage PHT. You agree to provide accurate information and to keep account credentials confidential. You are responsible for activity that occurs under your accounts.

Fees are set out in the applicable SOW or MSA. Recurring managed-service fees are billed monthly in advance unless otherwise stated. Project work is billed per the milestones defined in the SOW. Time & materials work is invoiced at our then-current hourly rates.

Invoices are due net 15 from the date of issue. Past-due balances accrue interest at 1.5% per month (or the maximum permitted by law, whichever is lower). PHT may suspend services for accounts more than 30 days past due.

To deliver our services, we need timely access to your environment, accurate documentation, and a designated point of contact. You agree to: (a) provide reasonable access to systems and facilities, (b) implement security recommendations we mark as critical, (c) maintain valid licenses for third-party software we manage, and (d) respond to requests for information needed to resolve incidents.
PHT is not liable for service interruptions or security incidents resulting from your failure to act on recommendations or maintain licensing.

Both parties may receive non-public information ("Confidential Information") in the course of an engagement. Each party will use the other's Confidential Information only to perform under the agreement, will protect it with the same care it uses for its own confidential data (at minimum reasonable care), and will not disclose it to third parties without prior written consent, except as required by law.

The Site, our methodologies, documentation templates, tooling, and any pre-existing materials remain the exclusive property of PHT. Materials created specifically for you under an SOW ("Work Product") are assigned to you upon full payment, subject to PHT's retained ownership of pre-existing IP and any third-party components.

PHT warrants that services will be performed in a professional and workmanlike manner consistent with industry standards. Except as expressly stated, the Site and all services are provided "as is" and "as available" without warranties of any kind, whether express, implied, or statutory, including without limitation warranties of merchantability, fitness for a particular purpose, and non-infringement.

To the maximum extent permitted by law, PHT's total aggregate liability arising out of or related to these Terms or our services will not exceed the fees paid by you to PHT during the three (3) months preceding the event giving rise to the claim. In no event will PHT be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages, including lost profits, lost data, or business interruption, even if advised of the possibility.

You will indemnify and hold PHT harmless from third-party claims arising out of (a) your use of the services in violation of these Terms or applicable law, (b) content or data you provide, or (c) your infringement of any third-party right.

Either party may terminate an engagement for material breach if the breach is not cured within thirty (30) days of written notice. Either party may terminate for convenience subject to the notice period defined in the applicable agreement (typically 30 days). Upon termination, you will pay all fees accrued through the termination date and we will reasonably assist with transition for up to thirty (30) days at our standard hourly rates.

These Terms are governed by the laws of the State of Utah, without regard to its conflict-of-law principles. Any disputes will be resolved in the state or federal courts located in Salt Lake County, Utah, and the parties consent to personal jurisdiction there.

We may update these Terms from time to time. Material changes will be posted on the Site with an updated "Last revised" date. Continued use of the Site or our services after the effective date constitutes acceptance.
Questions about these Terms? Email hello@primehourtech.com or call us during business hours.

contactCardCopy: Email hello@primehourtech.com or call us during business hours. We respond within one business day.

title: Privacy policy

children.text: We only collect what we need to talk to you, deliver services, and run the business. We don't sell your data, don't run behavioural ads, and don't train AI models on it. You can ask us what we hold, fix it, or delete it any time.
The detail below controls. The summary is a courtesy.

Prime Hour Tech LLC ("PHT," "we," "us," or "our") is a Utah-based managed services provider. This Privacy Policy explains what personal information we collect, how we use it, and the choices you have. It applies to this website (the "Site") and to information we receive when you contact us, request a proposal, or engage us for services.
Information processed under a signed Master Service Agreement (MSA) is also governed by the data-handling terms in that agreement. In the event of a conflict, the signed agreement controls.

We collect personal information in three ways: (1) You give it to us — when you fill out a form, request a call, email us, or sign an agreement: your name, business name, email, phone, role, and anything you include in the message. (2) We collect it automatically — when you visit the Site: IP address, device and browser characteristics, pages viewed, referring URL, and timestamps. We use first-party cookies and a small number of analytics cookies. (3) From third parties — if you arrive from a referral partner or advertising platform, we may receive limited information about how you found us.

We use the information we collect to: (a) respond to your inquiry and schedule discovery calls, (b) deliver, support, and bill for services under an active agreement, (c) maintain the security of our and our clients' systems, (d) comply with legal obligations, and (e) improve the Site and our services.
We do not sell personal information. We do not use your information to train third-party AI models. We do not run behavioural advertising on the Site.

Where data-protection law requires a legal basis for processing (e.g., GDPR), we rely on: contract performance (to provide services you requested), legitimate interests (to operate and secure the Site, respond to inquiries, manage client relationships), consent (for non-essential cookies and marketing emails — withdrawable at any time), and legal obligation (to keep records, respond to lawful requests).

We share information only with parties who help us run the business, under appropriate contractual terms. These include: cloud and hosting providers (Microsoft 365, Azure, our website host), payment processors and accounting software, remote-monitoring and ticketing platforms used to deliver managed services, and professional advisors (legal, accounting, insurance).

We disclose information when required by law, to enforce our agreements, or to protect rights, safety, and property. In the event of a corporate transaction (merger, acquisition, asset sale), information may transfer to the successor entity, subject to this Policy.

The Site uses two categories of cookies. Essential: required for navigation, security, and remembering your cookie choices — these are always on. Analytics: a privacy-respecting analytics tool we use to understand which pages are most useful. You can decline analytics cookies via the banner on your first visit. We do not use cross-site advertising trackers.

We keep personal information only as long as we need it for the purposes described above, plus the retention windows required by law and our insurance carriers. Baseline: inquiry contact records are retained for 24 months after the last interaction; client records and billing data for seven (7) years after the engagement ends; security and audit logs per our Information Security Policy schedule.

We protect personal information with administrative, technical, and physical safeguards aligned to the CIS Controls v8 framework: least-privilege access, MFA on all administrative accounts, encryption in transit and at rest, monitored endpoints, and documented incident-response procedures. No system is perfectly secure; we will notify affected individuals and clients of any incident as required by applicable law and our contracts.

Subject to applicable law, you may have the right to: access the personal information we hold about you, request correction of inaccurate information, request deletion, object to or restrict certain processing, and request a portable copy of information you provided.

To exercise any of these rights, email hello@primehourtech.com with the subject line "Privacy request." We will respond within 30 days and may need to verify your identity before acting on the request.

If you ask us to keep in touch, we may send occasional emails about our services and resources. Every message includes an unsubscribe link. Transactional emails (proposals, invoices, ticket updates, security advisories) are part of providing services and continue regardless of marketing preferences.
SMS messages require opt-in and are governed by our SMS Terms. Reply STOP to opt out at any time.

The Site and our services are not directed to children under 16, and we do not knowingly collect personal information from children. If you believe a child has provided information to us, contact us and we will delete it.

We are based in the United States and process information in the U.S. If you access the Site from outside the U.S., you understand your information is transferred to and processed in the U.S. under U.S. law, which may differ from the laws of your country.

We may update this Privacy Policy from time to time. Material changes will be posted on the Site with an updated "Last revised" date and, when appropriate, additional notice (e.g., a banner on the Site or email to active clients).
Questions about this Policy or our data practices? Email hello@primehourtech.com or call us during business hours. Mail: Prime Hour Tech LLC, Salt Lake City, UT.

contactCardCopy: Email hello@primehourtech.com with subject "Privacy request." We respond within 30 days.

title: About
seoTitle: About Prime Hour Tech | SLC Managed IT
seoDescription: Small IT team, big accountability. Salt Lake City-based managed services provider — named engineers, flat rates, and the founder on every quarterly review.

blocks.eyebrow: About · Est. 2024 · Salt Lake City, UT

children.text: treats your network like it's ours.

deck: We started Prime Hour Tech because we kept getting hired by businesses who'd been failed by an MSP that grew too fast, lost the engineers who knew their environment, and started routing tickets to call centers. We wanted to build the opposite of that.
Small, accountable, and answering the phone — for as long as we possibly can.

caption.quote: If we can't pick up your call, something has already gone wrong.

children.text: We've been on every side of

columns.heading: Enterprise IT — for over a decade.
columns.body: Before PHT, our founder ran infrastructure and security for organizations from 200 to 5,000 seats. Long migrations, late-night failovers, regulator audits — the unglamorous work that keeps a business open.

columns.heading: To keep small businesses from getting forgotten.
columns.body: We watched too many SMBs sign with MSPs that promised the world and then routed every call into a queue. We wanted a shop that stayed small enough that the engineer on your account today is still on it next year.

columns.heading: Flat-rate, line-itemed, no surprises.
columns.body: Every client has a named primary engineer, a named backup, and a quarterly business review with the founder. Tickets go to the same people. Pricing is on one page. The contract is one PDF.

blocks.deck: Two years in, deliberately small. Growing one named engineer at a time.

items.title: Prime Hour Tech is founded
items.body: Founder leaves a corporate infrastructure role. First two clients — a Salt Lake law firm and a multi-location dental practice — sign as design partners.

items.title: First engineer hired
items.body: Senior network and security engineer joins. The "named primary + named backup" model is formalized — every client gets two people who know their environment by heart.

items.title: Cybersecurity practice launches
items.body: CIS Controls v8-aligned baseline rolled out as standard for every managed client. Phishing simulation and incident-response retainer added as optional services.

items.title: Cloud + M365 administrator joins
items.body: Dedicated cloud admin brings M365 tenant and Azure migrations in-house. Helpdesk lead joins later that quarter — the four-person team that runs PHT today.

items.title: Quarterly business reviews go formal
items.body: Every client now gets a structured QBR with the founder: ticket data, security posture, license audit, and a 90-day roadmap.

blocks.heading: The same people. Every ticket.
blocks.deck: No call centers. No rotating account managers. You get to know us, we get to know your stack, and that's how problems get solved fast.

blocks.body: We're a single physical office in Sugar House, Salt Lake City. We don't operate satellite call centers, we don't outsource overnight tier-1, and we don't grow past the point where we can keep that promise. When it gets crowded, we hire the engineer first and figure out the desk second.
bullets: Engineers, not "account managers," are on every call.
On-site visits for any client within an hour of downtown SLC.
Quarterly office hours for clients — free for any working-stage problem.

blocks.heading: Six things we put in writing.
blocks.deck: Every one of these is in our MSA. Every breach is an automatic credit.
items: We tell you the truth, even when it costs us a sale.
We document everything. Nothing lives in one person's head.
We treat your network like it's ours.
We bill flat. No mystery line items.
We assume the internet is hostile. We build accordingly.

children.text: Coffee, a tour of the office,

blocks.deck: All three work. We'll take 30 minutes to look at your stack and tell you whether PHT fits — even if the honest answer is 'not yet'.

title: Industries
seoTitle: Industries We Serve | Prime Hour Tech SLC
seoDescription: Managed IT and cybersecurity for professional services, SMBs, regulated industries, and nonprofits in Salt Lake City. Flat-rate, named engineers.

deck: The baseline is the same — flat-rate, named engineers, real humans on the phone. What changes is which controls we prioritize, which software we integrate with, and which regulators show up on quarterly review.
Below: the four verticals that make up most of our book. Don't see yours? Most of what we do crosses categories cleanly.

verticals.name: Professional Services
verticals.intro: Firms that bill by the hour can't afford downtime, and the data on their drives is often subject to confidentiality rules that go beyond ordinary best practice. We design for both.
bullets: Secure document management and file sharing

verticals.name: Small & Mid-Size Business
verticals.intro: The sweet spot for managed IT — big enough that ad-hoc support has stopped working, small enough that a CIO is a stretch. We slot in between.
bullets: Predictable monthly billing — no hourly tab
Strategic IT planning and quarterly QBRs
Fast helpdesk with named primary engineers

verticals.name: Regulated Industries
verticals.intro: Regulators give you a checklist; auditors give you evidence requirements. We map our baseline to both so the conversation with your compliance lead is short.
bullets: SOC-2-friendly evidence packs aligned to CIS v8
CMMC / DFARS readiness for GovCon clients

verticals.name: Nonprofits & Education
verticals.intro: Nonprofit budgets are tight and grant rules are picky. We help with Microsoft / Google nonprofit programs, tax-exempt licensing, and the audit trail funders expect.
bullets: Donor-data protection (HIPAA-style controls for sensitive cases)

blocks.deck: The categories above are where we have deepest pattern recognition. If your business doesn't fit cleanly into one, it usually fits cleanly into the cross-section of two. Tell us what you do — we'll tell you whether we're a fit.
30 minutes with the founder. We'll look at your environment and tell you honestly whether PHT is a fit — no matter what industry you're in.

ctaSecondary.label: See how we save you money

blocks.heading: We stop the IT headaches keeping you up at night.

items.pain: Your IT person is out and nobody knows the passwords

title: Services
seoTitle: IT Services for SLC Businesses | Prime Hour Tech
seoDescription: Six IT services. One Salt Lake City team. Managed IT, cybersecurity, cloud, infrastructure, vCIO advisory, and compliance — flat-rate, named engineers.

children.text: run by the people you'll meet.

blocks.deck: Six services. One team in Salt Lake City. Managed IT and Cybersecurity are the spine of most engagements; the other four plug in where you need them — a cloud migration, a project, a quarterly QBR, a website that quietly keeps working.
All prices per seat per month, billed monthly. No setup fees, no after-hours surcharges, no ticket caps. Organizations over 100 seats are custom-priced — same model, different math.

essentials.tagline: For small teams that just need the lights to stay on. Helpdesk, monitoring, and patching — flat-rate.

standard.tagline: Where most SMBs land. Managed IT plus the cybersecurity baseline we'd want on our own environment.

premier.tagline: For organizations that have outgrown a part-time admin and need strategy, not just support.

blocks.deck: Most engagements start with Managed IT + Cybersecurity, then layer Cloud or vCIO as the business grows. We price it as one flat monthly — no per-service line items.

tiles.name: Cybersecurity
tiles.descriptor: CIS v8-aligned endpoint, email, identity, and incident response.
tiles.includesLine: EDR · Email security · MFA · Phishing sim · Incident response

tiles.name: Cloud Services
tiles.descriptor: Microsoft 365 and Azure tenant administration, migrations, and license audits.
tiles.includesLine: M365 admin · Azure · Migrations · License audits · Teams rollouts

tiles.name: Infrastructure
tiles.descriptor: Fixed-fee projects — office moves, network rebuilds, server replacements.
tiles.includesLine: Office moves · Network rebuilds · Server refresh · Runbook included

tiles.includesLine: Gap assessment · HIPAA · SOC 2 readiness · CIS v8 · Evidence pack

tiles.name: Bundle them
tiles.descriptor: Most clients run Managed IT + Cybersecurity as the spine, then add services as they grow.
tiles.includesLine: One monthly rate · One invoice · One team · No per-service line items

blocks.deck: Whether it's a 3-week project or a 5-year managed relationship, the operating model is the same: a named engineer who owns your account, monthly check-ins, quarterly reviews, and a written runbook you can take with you if we ever lose the trust to keep it.

steps.title: Discovery
steps.body: A 30-minute call. We look at your environment, your support load, and tell you honestly whether we're a fit.

steps.title: Written SOW
steps.body: Scope and price in writing before anyone touches a wire. Change requests are also in writing — never verbal.

steps.title: Named engineer
steps.body: One primary on your account from day one. Their photo, direct line, and email — not a generic queue.

steps.title: QBR + runbook
steps.body: Quarterly business review with the founder. Annual budget. A runbook you own and can leave us with.

blocks.deck: 30 minutes with the founder. We'll look at your environment, your team, and where things are quietly costing you. Then we'll tell you which of the six actually fit — even if the answer is not yet.

title: Switching from your current MSP
seoTitle: Switching MSPs in Salt Lake City | Prime Hour Tech
seoDescription: Switching MSPs is less painful than staying. Prime Hour Tech handles the notice letter, credential handover, and side-by-side coverage. 30-day migration, 0 coverage gap.

blocks.deck: Most of our SLC clients came to us off another MSP — and the migration is rarely what they were dreading. We handle the awkward parts (the notice letter, the credential handover, the side-by-side coverage) so your team mostly notices the calls start getting answered faster.

stats.v: Own every credential & doc at the end

blocks.deck: When a Salt Lake City business owner finally calls us about switching, the conversation almost always lands on one of these. The first is the most common.

items.head: The MSP grew faster than its bench.
items.body: You signed when they had 12 engineers. They have 60 now, half of whom you haven't met. Your ticket gets re-assigned three times before anyone touches it.

items.head: The invoice keeps surprising you.
items.body: Hourly billing on top of "managed" services, project fees that weren't in writing, after-hours surcharges. You stopped opening invoices in the inbox preview pane.

items.head: The engineer who knew you left.
items.body: Your account was held together by one person who understood your stack. They left. The handoff was an email. The new engineer keeps asking you for context the last engineer should have written down.

blocks.deck: We run every migration on the same calendar. There's a discovery call, a written runbook, and side-by-side coverage from day one. You should never feel like there's a gap between old MSP and new MSP.

phases.title: Discovery, decision, paperwork.
phases.body: A 30-minute call with the founder. We look at your environment, your support load, and your current MSP's contract. If we're a fit, you get a written SOW with a fixed monthly price before anyone signs anything. · Discovery call notes and templated notice letter delivered to you.

phases.title: Documentation, side-by-side, named engineer.
phases.body: We send notice to your current MSP and request credential and documentation handover. While that lands, we begin our own environment walkthrough — networking, identity, endpoints, backups. You are introduced to your named engineer; their direct line and email go into your team's contacts. · Inventory document live, side-by-side coverage in place.

phases.title: Baseline deployed. The gaps get closed.
phases.body: Monitoring agents, EDR, and MFA enforcement roll out across endpoints — usually overnight, in waves. We close the security gaps the previous MSP didn't or didn't want to talk about. Backups are validated with a real restore, not a screenshot. · EDR plus monitoring deployed, MFA enforced, backup restore verified.

phases.title: Old MSP off. Steady state on.
phases.body: The previous MSP's access is revoked, their tools removed, their domain accounts disabled. The first monthly summary lands. The QBR with the founder is on the calendar. You have a runbook on your drive that you own — if you ever leave us, it leaves with you. · Previous MSP fully off-boarded, runbook delivered (markdown + PDF).

blocks.deck: Most owners delay switching MSPs because they're bracing for an awkward set of conversations. We handle nearly all of them for you. You sign a few things. That's it.

items.painBody: Writing the notice letter to your current MSP feels awkward — you don't want to burn a bridge but you need out.
items.weHandleBody: A clean, professional letter ready to send. We give you the draft; you send it on your letterhead. No drama.

items.painBody: Getting admin passwords, certificate stores, and vault access back from the old MSP is a coordination headache most owners dread.
items.weHandleBody: We coordinate the privileged-access transfer directly with their team — admin accounts, password vaults, certificate stores. You stay out of it.

items.painBody: Runbooks and configs often live only with the outgoing MSP. If they refuse to hand them over, you're left rebuilding from scratch.
items.weHandleBody: A formal request goes out for the runbooks and configs they have on file. If they refuse, we rebuild from scratch and document better. Either way, you end up with a runbook you own.

items.painBody: During a switchover there is usually a window where nobody is quite sure who handles a ticket — the old MSP or the new one.
items.weHandleBody: During the handover window, both MSPs cover tickets. Your team never has a who-do-I-call moment. We run the overlap deliberately so there is no dead zone.

items.painBody: The old MSP's monitoring agents, RMM clients, and remote-access tools stay on endpoints until someone audits and removes them — which almost never happens automatically.
items.weHandleBody: Their monitoring agents, RMM clients, and remote-access tools come off every endpoint, audited and verified. We re-point your Comcast, Microsoft, and line-of-business vendor accounts to PHT as the technical contact.

rows.them: Hourly + project fees on top of "managed"

rows.us: One named engineer. Their photo, line, email.
A runbook you own. Markdown + PDF.
All in Salt Lake & Utah Valley.

promises.head: No coverage gap.
promises.body: Side-by-side coverage from the day you sign through the day the previous MSP is fully off-boarded. Your team never wonders who to call.

promises.head: You own your runbook.
promises.body: Markdown + PDF handed over at the end of every migration. If you ever leave us, the runbook leaves with you — that's the deal.

promises.head: No multi-year lock-in.
promises.body: Month-to-month after the first 90 days. If we're not earning the seat every month, you should be able to walk.

promises.head: You meet the team.
promises.body: You're introduced to your named engineer in week one. The founder is on every QBR. No phantom "account managers" in between.

blocks.quote: I'd been told switching MSPs was a four-month nightmare. It wasn't. They ran their thing in parallel with our old MSP for two weeks, and by the time we cut over, my team didn't even notice — except that tickets started getting answered the same day.
blocks.attributionContext: Professional services firm · Salt Lake City · 42 seats

items.question: Will my team experience downtime during the switch?
items.answer: No — that's the whole point of side-by-side coverage. Until the old MSP is off-boarded, both teams have access. Your end-users don't notice a cut-over day; they notice that tickets start getting answered faster.

items.question: What if my current MSP won't hand over documentation?
items.answer: It happens. Their contract usually says they own the runbooks. We assume that and rebuild from scratch — and frankly, we document better than most of the MSPs we replace. The handover request still goes; we just don't depend on it.

items.question: I'm in a multi-year contract. Can I still switch?
items.answer: Usually yes. Most MSP contracts have a 60- or 90-day notice clause buried in them. We'll read your contract with you on the discovery call and tell you what your real exit window is.

items.question: How do you handle credentials and admin access?
items.answer: Via a formal privileged-access handover — coordinated directly between our security lead and their senior engineer. Old credentials are rotated; new ones go into your vault, which you own. Nothing sits in a Slack channel.

items.question: What does the transition cost?
items.answer: Onboarding is included in the first month's flat rate for migrations under 100 seats. Larger transitions get a one-time onboarding fee, quoted in writing on the SOW. There are no per-hour surprises.

items.question: What if we decide you're not the right fit halfway through?
items.answer: You walk. The first 90 days exist specifically for this — both sides should know if it's working. If you leave in that window, you take the runbook with you and we hand back the keys cleanly. No drama.

blocks.eyebrow: Ready to talk about your switch?
blocks.deck: 30 minutes with the founder. Bring your current MSP's contract if you have it handy — we'll read the exit clause with you and tell you what your real timeline looks like. No pressure. If we're not the right fit, we'll tell you who is.
